AttributeReference.description: Define one or more Kafka bootstrap broker hosts and ports. Use the broker address provided by Confluent, Redpanda, Aiven, IBM Event Streams or your Kafka administrator.
Select Internet for public Kafka endpoints or On-Premise when routing through SAP Cloud Connector.
SAP Cloud Connector Location ID. Required only when Proxy Type is On-Premise and the Cloud Connector uses a Location ID.
Select the Kafka authentication profile. NONE for unauthenticated brokers. SASL for PLAIN, SCRAM-SHA-256 or SCRAM-SHA-512 — with or without TLS.
Enable TLS/SSL encryption for the broker connection. ON = SASL_SSL for SASL. OFF = SASL_PLAINTEXT for SASL without TLS; credential alias is still required.
SASL mechanism used by the broker. PLAIN is common for Confluent Cloud; SCRAM depends on broker configuration.
Security Material alias containing Kafka username/password or secure parameters required by the selected authentication profile.
Controls whether the adapter uses the JVM/system public CA trust store or a custom private CA certificate alias from the CPI Keystore. Required when TLS is enabled with a private CA.
CPI Keystore alias for the custom broker CA certificate. Required only when Broker CA = Custom.
Kafka topic name, comma-separated topic list, or supported topic pattern depending on the adapter mode. Example: acme.br.sales.order.created.
Kafka consumer group ID used to store committed offsets. Keep stable for normal consumption. Use a new group only for isolated replay tests.
Select automatic Kafka consumer-group assignment or explicit fixed partition assignment.
Comma-separated partition list used only in Explicit Partition(s) mode. Example: 0,1,2.
Number of consumer instances created by the adapter. Effective parallelism is limited by the number of topic partitions.
Kafka reset policy used only when the group has no committed offset. Existing committed offsets take precedence over earliest/latest/none.
Runtime policy for failed records. Skip commits the failed offset and continues. Stop does not commit and halts. Retry does not commit and retries according to retry settings.
Number of pipeline retry attempts for Retry Failed Message. The failed Kafka offset is not committed until processing succeeds.
Fetch size profile in MB. Fixed values only: 1, 5, 10 or 20 MB. No free-text input.
Maximum fetch size in MB. Fixed values only: 1, 5, 10 or 20 MB.
Maximum number of Kafka records returned per poll. Use 1 for deterministic troubleshooting; use higher values for batch consumption.
Disabled for normal consumption. Seek by Timestamp for controlled replay/recovery using the topic, partition and group ID configured in Processing.
Format of the recovery timestamp. Auto Detect accepts ISO-8601 UTC from Confluent, Unix epoch milliseconds, or Unix epoch seconds.
Timestamp used for recovery. Examples: 2026-06-07T14:19:07.121Z, 1780841947121, or 1780841947.
Read only first matched message stops after the first record resolved by the timestamp. Continue from timestamp replays from that point forward.
Select payload conversion mode. None passes raw bytes. JSON/XML convert Avro payloads using Schema Registry settings.
Schema Registry base URL used for Avro conversion. Example: https://psrc-xxxxx.us-east-2.aws.confluent.cloud.
Security Material alias containing Schema Registry credentials.
Magic Byte reads the schema ID from Confluent wire format. Fixed Schema ID forces a configured schema ID.
Fixed Schema Registry ID used only when Schema ID Source is Fixed Schema ID.
Maximum payload size accepted for conversion, in MB. Kafka may fetch larger records, but conversion fails if this limit is exceeded.
Number of bytes inspected at the beginning of the payload for schema/magic-byte diagnostics.
Maximum time in seconds for broker/client request operations before timeout.
Maximum Kafka client reconnect/retry backoff in milliseconds.
Delay in seconds before retrying broker initialization after retryable startup failures.
Kafka consumer session timeout in seconds. Lower values detect dead consumers faster, but too low can cause rebalances.
Maximum interval between poll calls. Keep lower than Kafka default to avoid long zombie-like rebalance waits in CPI tests.
Kafka bootstrap broker host name. Do not include protocol. Example: pkc-xxxxx.us-east-2.aws.confluent.cloud.
Kafka bootstrap broker port. Common secure Kafka port is 9092.

GuiLabels.Label: 📜 Custom — Private CA from CPI Keystore
Max. Fetch Wait Time (hidden runtime default)
